name: Azure Identity (Microsoft Authentication Library for Python)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Microsoft — authentication for Azure services

auth.notes: Supports managed identity, workload identity, service principal, device code, browser, CLI auth

performance.notes: Token caching built-in; refresh on expiry; minimal overhead for hot paths

agent_readiness.notes: Unified credential chain for all Azure SDK libraries. from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential, ManagedIdentityCredential, ClientSecretCredential. credential = DefaultAzureCredential(). client = SecretClient(vault_url=..., credential=credential). DefaultAzureCredential chains: env vars, workload identity, managed identity, Azure CLI, VS Code. Production: ManagedIdentityCredential. Service principal: ClientSecretCredential(tenant, client_id, secret). Workload Identity for AKS pods. Async: DefaultAzureCredential(). Works with all azure-* SDK packages. Token cache auto-refresh.

gotchas: DefaultAzureCredential tries 8+ auth methods in order — debugging which one fires can be confusing
Local development requires Azure CLI login or env vars
Workload Identity in AKS requires additional cluster configuration
Token expiry logging can be noisy without proper log filtering
best_when: Any Python code accessing Azure services (Key Vault, Storage, OpenAI, Cosmos)
Production Azure workloads using Managed Identity (no secrets in code)
AKS pods with Workload Identity federation
Multi-environment code that works locally (CLI auth) and in production (Managed Identity)
avoid_when: Non-Azure cloud (use boto3 credential chain for AWS, google-auth for GCP)
Simple client_credentials OAuth without Azure resources
summary: azure-identity provides a unified credential chain for all Azure SDK libraries. DefaultAzureCredential() auto-detects: env vars → workload identity → managed identity → Azure CLI → VS Code. credential = DefaultAzureCredential(); client = SecretClient(vault_url, credential=credential). Production: ManagedIdentityCredential. Service principal: ClientSecretCredential(tenant, client_id, secret). Token caching with auto-refresh. Works with all azure-* SDK packages. Essential for any Python + Azure integration.
